module.name: src
module.purpose: Rust workspace source root for Marix crates with flat Cargo crate layout. It organizes reusable common config/external/logging/protocol/structure modules, agent model/session/task orchestration, client session/event surfaces, host-side tool execution, and the top-level tool crate. The agent session runtime keeps SessionContext in SessionState, resets it when a host connects, queries host tool previews with ExecutionEvent::PreviewQuery, and stores returned Preview tools; the host executor answers PreviewQuery by emitting ExecutionEvent::Preview with registry previews. The common protocol namespace includes SessionEvent variants for Task, Step, and Execution, TaskPreview, TaskRequestBrief, ToolSchema, and ToolPreview.

childModules.name: agent
childModules.purpose: Agent crate namespace for model backend abstractions, Session and Task runtime orchestration, plain state storage with SessionContext, host-preview bootstrap/query handling, Preview tool storage, and safe ignored routing for standalone Step events.

childModules.name: client
childModules.purpose: Client crate namespace for the user-facing ClientSession and ClientEvent surface. ClientSession is now implemented as a self-connecting client that mirrors HostSession: new(name) stores the session name in a SOURCE_NAME static, constructs it and immediately spawns a worker (moving the user_tx channel half into it) that loads config.client.core_address, connect_channel::<SessionMessage> to the agent (retrying), stores the NetSender into the shared agent_tx, and runs a current-thread tokio runtime that unwraps each inbound agent SessionMessage and translates message.event into user-facing ClientEvents (content-bearing task events only, via to_client_event); is_connected() reports whether the worker has established the shared agent_tx NetSender, and the marix-client binary polls it in a bounded wait after new() so a create_task/cancel_task is not issued before that sender exists; create_task and cancel_task package task commands via the static package_message(SessionEvent) -> SessionMessage and send them to the agent through the shared agent_tx (SharedNetSender<SessionMessage>), receiver() borrows the ClientEvent stream (&Receiver<ClientEvent> over user_rx) for the caller to consume, and close() signals the shutdown flag and stops the worker. query_task was removed, the previously public user_rx field is now private, and the user_tx sender half is no longer a struct field (new() moves it into the worker); marix-client now depends on tokio (net, rt-multi-thread, sync, time) for the recv runtime. The crate now also ships the marix-client binary entry (main.rs) that loads Config, constructs a ClientSession::new(config.name) and waits via is_connected() until it is ready, and runs one-shot or interactive prompts, draining ClientEvent::Common messages until idle.

childModules.name: common
childModules.purpose: Shared common crate namespace with reusable config, external re-export, logging, protocol, and structure modules. Its protocol module groups TaskId, ExeId, SessionEvent (Task, Step, Execution), TaskPreview, TaskRequestBrief, ToolSchema, and ToolPreview in dedicated child-module files alongside step and execution contracts.

childModules.name: host
childModules.purpose: Host crate namespace for HostSession and host-side tool execution. HostSession connects to the agent and routes inbound execution events through Executor; Executor owns the tool registry, active execution runtimes, preview delegation, PreviewQuery response emission, Evoke creation, and forwarding for other execution events.

childModules.name: tool
childModules.purpose: Top-level marix-tool crate (lib marix_tool) that defines the per-tool ToolProgram interface and compiles one executable per native tool program (read_file, write_file, list_directory, search_text, shell_execute), each selected at build time via a Cargo feature and sharing the tool_main.rs --preview/--run entry. The Executor-side Tool handle that launches these executables lives in the host crate (host/executor), not in this crate. Every native tool program's invoke() is now implemented, parsing the JSON call and performing real filesystem/shell IO to return JSON results (no longer panic stubs).
